summary: I was born in Thailand and spent much of my childhood there. I lived in different places including Mae Ra Moe camp and Mae Sariang city. Thailand is where my family and I lived before we applied to move to the United States in 2009.

I studied at Mae Ra Moe camp in section four starting from kindergarten when I was five years old. I attended school there from first grade through seventh grade. After applying to move to the United States, I returned to Mae Ra Moe camp several times for interviews and to wait before moving on to Mae Sot and eventually leaving Thailand.

During summer school in middle school, I lived with my dad in Mae Sariang city in Thailand. I enjoyed swimming in the Mae Sariang River, which made me very happy. It was a special time for me while I was still studying in Thailand.

My family and I moved to the United States in May 2011 after applying in 2009. We arrived in Saint Paul, Minnesota, where we met our sponsors and caseworkers. I started attending Washington school in September 2011, beginning seventh grade and studying subjects like English, math, science, and social studies.

Mae Sot was an important place in our journey to the United States. We went there for medical checks that lasted seven days and later returned for training before traveling to Bangkok and then leaving Thailand. Mae Sot was a key stop between Mae Ra Moe camp and our departure.

When we left Thailand, we flew to Japan first before continuing on to the United States. We changed airplanes in Japan during our long journey. It was part of the travel route from Bangkok to Los Angeles and then to Minnesota.

Los Angeles was another stop on our way to Minnesota. After flying from Japan, we changed airplanes in Los Angeles before continuing to Saint Paul. It was part of the long journey that brought us to our new home in the United States.

We arrived in Saint Paul, Minnesota on May 24, 2011, where we were warmly welcomed by our caseworker, nephew, and sponsors. This is where we started our new life in the United States. I began school there in September and attended Washington school starting in seventh grade.

Scott Pearcy and his family were among the sponsors who greeted us at the airport in Saint Paul when we arrived. They helped us as we settled into our new home. I remember taking a photo with them and the other sponsor families that day.

Bob Lein and his family were also sponsors who welcomed us at the airport in Saint Paul. Their support was important as we adjusted to life in the United States. We took a group photo together with all the sponsors when we arrived.

Cherry Puls and her family were part of the group of sponsors who met us when we landed in Saint Paul. Their kindness helped us feel welcomed in our new country. We all took a photo together at the airport as a memory of that day.

Brenda Minette and her family were among the sponsors who greeted us upon our arrival in Saint Paul, Minnesota. Their help was crucial during our first days in the United States. I remember taking a photo with all the sponsors and feeling grateful for their support.
